meaning: A phrase used to tell someone that their microphone is turned off during a call.
example: We can see your lips moving, but you're muted; please turn on your microphone.

meaning: To show the contents of your computer screen to other participants in a virtual meeting.
example: Let me share my screen so you can see the marketing graphics we prepared.

meaning: Hesitant or unwilling to turn on your webcam during a video call.
example: Most of the team was camera shy today, so it was mostly an audio call.

meaning: A smaller, separate virtual room created during a larger online meeting for small group discussions.
example: The host split us into breakout rooms to brainstorm ideas in smaller groups.

meaning: A delay in sound transmission during a call, usually caused by a poor internet connection.
example: There is a significant audio lag, making it hard to have a smooth conversation.

meaning: To leave a virtual meeting or call before it has finished.
example: I need to drop off this call five minutes early to attend another meeting.

meaning: To quickly join a virtual meeting or call.
example: Can you hop on a quick call so we can resolve this design issue together?

meaning: To join a virtual meeting using a telephone instead of a computer internet connection.
example: If your Wi-Fi is weak, you can dial in using the phone number in the invitation.

meaning: A fixed time when a meeting must end or a participant must leave due to another commitment.
example: I have a hard stop at 3:00 PM, so we must finish our discussion by then.

meaning: Distracting sounds in a person's physical environment during a call.
example: Please mute yourself if you have background noise like traffic or construction.

meaning: A repetition of sound during a call caused by audio feedback from speakers.
example: I hear an echo when you speak; please try using headphones.

meaning: A list of topics and items to be discussed during a meeting.
example: I will send out the meeting agenda so everyone can prepare their updates.

meaning: A short, light activity at the start of a meeting to help participants feel comfortable.
example: We started our virtual meeting with a fun icebreaker about our favorite foods.

meaning: Time spent interacting with colleagues directly, either in person or via video call.
example: Even though we work remotely, getting some face time on video calls helps build trust.

meaning: A brief summary of the main points and decisions discussed in a meeting.
example: I will send a quick recap of today's call along with the next steps.

meaning: To schedule two different meetings or events at the same time.
example: I apologize, I am double-booked at 10 AM, so I must reschedule our chat.

meaning: The first meeting with a project team to align on goals and start the project.
example: We will hold a kickoff meeting on Monday to introduce the new client project.

meaning: A meeting held after a project is finished to analyze what went well and what went wrong.
example: During the project postmortem, we discussed how to avoid timeline delays in the future.

meaning: A quick update during a meeting to see how a specific task is progressing.
example: Let's do a quick status check on the website launch before we move to marketing.

meaning: The person who organizes, starts, and manages a virtual meeting.
example: As the host of the meeting, please record the session for those who couldn't attend.

meaning: To send a short message to someone online to get their attention.
example: Ping me on Slack when you are free to discuss the budget.

meaning: A series of connected messages on a specific topic within a chat application.
example: Please reply in the thread so we do not clutter the main channel.

meaning: A short warning or advance notice about something.
example: Just a heads up, the website will undergo maintenance tonight.

meaning: A private message sent directly to one person instead of a group channel.
example: Send me a direct message with your email address.

meaning: To silence notifications from a specific chat channel or group.
example: I muted the random channel so I can focus on my core tasks.

meaning: To mention a user using '@' to send them a direct notification in chat.
example: If you need immediate help, tag the project manager in your message.

meaning: An instance of referencing someone directly in an online message.
example: I missed your mention because I was away from my laptop.

meaning: An indicator showing that a user is currently not active at their computer.
example: Her away status suggests she has stepped out for lunch.

meaning: A setting that blocks all incoming notifications to allow focused work.
example: I put my status on Do Not Disturb while writing the presentation.

meaning: An audio message recorded and sent in a chat application.
example: He sent a quick voice note to explain the issue while walking.

meaning: Using a small icon to respond to a message without writing text.
example: Give me a thumbs-up emoji reaction once you have read this.

meaning: A dedicated chat room for a specific team, topic, or project.
example: We created a new chat channel specifically for the marketing campaign.

meaning: To attach a message to the top of a chat or channel so it is easy to find.
example: The links to the design guidelines are pinned to the top of the channel.

meaning: Not connected to the internet or not available on messaging apps.
example: He is offline for the day, so he will answer your message tomorrow.

meaning: Connected to the internet and active on messaging applications.
example: She is online right now, so you can ask her for help.

meaning: A small spelling error made when typing a message.
example: Sorry for the typo; I meant 'send the file,' not 'lend the file.'

meaning: A status or automatic reply showing that an employee is away from work.
example: I set my Out Of Office reply because I am taking a vacation next week.

meaning: A phrase used to show you have received and understood a message.
example: Copy that, I will update the project board immediately.

meaning: An indicator showing that a recipient has viewed a sent message.
example: She saw your message because the read receipt is visible.

meaning: A short custom message next to your name showing your current task or state.
example: He changed his status update to 'In a Meeting' so he wouldn't be interrupted.

meaning: To give someone responsibility for a task or project.
example: The manager will assign the research task to the marketing team.

meaning: A visual tool used to organize and track tasks in a project.
example: Please check the project board to see which tasks are still pending.

meaning: A digital record used to track a specific request, task, or bug report.
example: I submitted a support ticket to get my password reset.

meaning: A list of future tasks or project requirements that are waiting to be done.
example: We put the new feature request into the project backlog for next month.

meaning: A short, set period of time (typically two weeks) to complete specific tasks.
example: We have several high-priority tasks to deliver in this sprint.

meaning: Unable to make progress on a task due to waiting for someone or something else.
example: I am blocked on writing the report because I don't have the final numbers.

meaning: To remove an obstacle so that progress on a task can continue.
example: Thank you for sending the data; that unblocks my analysis task.

meaning: A final product, document, or service that must be completed and handed over.
example: The design prototype is our main deliverable for this week.

meaning: A specific task assigned to an individual during a meeting to be completed later.
example: Our team has three action items to complete before the next review.

meaning: An important event or key stage in the progress of a project.
example: Releasing the beta version was a major milestone for our team.

meaning: The sequence of steps a task goes through from start to finish.
example: We need to define a clear workflow for review and approval of content.

meaning: Passing responsibility for a project or task to another person.
example: I completed the handover process before leaving for my vacation.

meaning: A quick, daily meeting where team members share progress and issues.
example: In our morning standup, I reported that my code changes are complete.

meaning: To meet or talk to ensure everyone has the same information and goals.
example: Let's sync up tomorrow morning to align our project plans.

meaning: A brief meeting or message to verify progress or see how someone is doing.
example: I will do a quick check-in with the client to share our progress.

meaning: The date or time by which a task must be completed.
example: The due date for submitting the draft proposal is this Friday.

meaning: Currently being worked on and not yet finished.
example: The website redesign is currently in progress and looking great.

meaning: To write or create a document together with one or more people.
example: We will co-author the guide on a shared cloud document.

meaning: The person who is responsible for ensuring a task is finished.
example: As the task owner, you must update the status on the board.

meaning: A schedule showing when tasks in a project are planned to happen.
example: The project timeline shows we have three weeks left before the launch.

meaning: To discuss a topic again at a later time.
example: Let's circle back to the budget discussion during next week's meeting.

meaning: To briefly contact or meet with someone to exchange information.
example: I want to touch base with you tomorrow about the feedback.

meaning: To discuss a topic privately outside of a group meeting or channel.
example: Let's take this technical discussion offline so we do not waste group time.

meaning: To include someone in an ongoing discussion or email thread.
example: Please loop in the marketing director so she is aware of these changes.

meaning: To keep someone informed about a situation or project updates.
example: Please keep me in the loop regarding the client's decision.

meaning: Describing a general overview without entering into small details.
example: He gave a high-level summary of the new software platform.

meaning: An in-depth, detailed analysis or discussion of a specific subject.
example: We need to do a deep dive into the user data to find the bug.

meaning: Highly detailed or focusing on small, specific elements.
example: Let's look at the customer feedback at a more granular level.

meaning: Providing clear information or guidance that can be acted upon immediately.
example: Please make sure your feedback is actionable so we know what to change.

meaning: Being aware of something that will need attention in the future.
example: Yes, the upcoming audit is on my radar; I am preparing files for it.

meaning: The time, energy, or mental capacity available to handle additional tasks.
example: I do not have the bandwidth to take on another project right now.

meaning: Easy tasks that can be completed quickly to achieve fast results.
example: Updating the text on the homepage is low-hanging fruit for the team.

meaning: To delay a deadline, or to oppose a decision or request.
example: The engineering team had to push back the delivery date by one week.

meaning: To agree on goals, plans, or directions with other team members.
example: We need to align with the sales team before we launch the product.

meaning: A recommended method or procedure that consistently produces the best results.
example: Documenting your code is a best practice that helps the whole team.

meaning: A key point or lesson learned from a discussion, meeting, or event.
example: My main takeaway from the workshop is that we must simplify our design.

meaning: Happening immediately, with no delay in communication or processing.
example: We can collaborate in real time on this document using cloud software.

meaning: Communication where messages are sent and replied to at different times.
example: Asynchronous communication allows us to work smoothly across time zones.

meaning: To isolate a team or information from the rest of the organization.
example: We should avoid working in silos; sharing information helps everyone.

meaning: Agreement on and support for a proposed idea or plan from stakeholders.
example: We need to get the manager's buy-in before we implement the new workflow.

meaning: A global network of remote servers used to store and manage data online.
example: All project files are saved in the cloud so anyone can access them.

meaning: An online storage space accessible by multiple users in an organization.
example: Please upload the client contracts to the department's shared drive.

meaning: To give someone permission to view, edit, or use a file or system.
example: Can you grant access to the designer so they can edit the document?

meaning: Settings that control what a user is allowed to do in a file or software.
example: You need to change the permissions so the client can only view the sheet.

meaning: To record an event, action, or work hours in a digital system.
example: Don't forget to log your working hours in the app before Friday evening.

meaning: A video capture of a computer screen, often with voice explanations.
example: I sent a quick screen recording to show the developer how the error happens.

meaning: An image capture of a computer or phone screen.
example: Please take a screenshot of the error message and send it to IT support.

meaning: A keyboard shortcut used to perform an action quickly.
example: Using the hotkey to mute myself during calls is very convenient.

meaning: To reload a webpage or application to display the latest updates.
example: If the new folder does not appear, try to refresh your browser.

meaning: To restart a computer or device to resolve performance problems.
example: My webcam is not working, so I need to reboot my laptop.

meaning: Software designed to help people work together on tasks and projects remotely.
example: Slack, Zoom, and Trello are essential collaboration tools for our team.

meaning: A temporary folder where outgoing messages are held before they are sent.
example: My email is stuck in the outbox because my internet disconnected.

meaning: The primary folder where incoming emails and messages are received.
example: I came back from vacation to find over one hundred messages in my inbox.

meaning: To store old messages or files in a separate space to keep active areas organized.
example: I archive completed project channels to keep my workspace clean.

meaning: A sound or visual pop-up informing you of a new message or update.
example: I turned off notification alerts on my phone to avoid distractions.

meaning: The digital setup or platform where a team interacts and shares files.
example: Welcome to our digital workspace; please check the onboarding channel.

meaning: Connections between different apps that allow them to share data automatically.
example: We set up integrations to receive task updates directly in Slack.

meaning: A URL that points straight to a specific page, folder, or asset.
example: Here is the direct link to the folder containing the project images.

meaning: A search box where you can type keywords to find items in an app.
example: Type the client's name in the search bar to locate past messages.

meaning: A digital switch that changes between two settings (e.g., on and off).
example: You can toggle the dark mode option in the settings menu.

meaning: Measures and practices to protect systems, networks, and data from attacks.
example: The company holds annual cyber security training to prevent phishing.

meaning: A period when you avoid using computers, phones, or other screens to rest.
example: I am doing a digital detox this weekend and will not check my email.

meaning: Sending or giving access to digital files to other users.
example: File sharing is simple and secure on our cloud platform.

meaning: A duplicate file created to protect against loss of the original data.
example: I keep a backup copy of all my presentations on an external drive.

meaning: A notification that a program or system has stopped working unexpectedly.
example: The design software crashed, but fortunately my work was auto-saved.

meaning: The progress of updating data across different devices to make them match.
example: Please wait for the sync status to show completed before closing the app.

meaning: An improvement to software that adds features, fixes bugs, or improves security.
example: You need to run a system update before installing the new project manager tool.

meaning: A period when a system, network, or server is not running or available.
example: The server down time is scheduled for Sunday morning to avoid disrupting work.

meaning: The state of having an empty email inbox, representing organized communication.
example: Achieving inbox zero before leaving for the weekend feels amazing.

meaning: An unauthorized release of private or sensitive digital information.
example: The company uses data encryption to prevent any accidental data leak.

meaning: An organizational culture where working remotely is the primary option for all staff.
example: Our company is remote-first, so all meetings are designed for online participants.

meaning: A work model that combines working from home and working in a physical office.
example: Many companies offer a hybrid work model where staff come in two days a week.

meaning: A person who works remotely while traveling and living in different places.
example: As a digital nomad, he spends his winters working from warm coastal towns.

meaning: Sharing an office space with other self-employed people or remote workers.
example: She goes to a coworking hub twice a week to socialize and network.

meaning: A designated block of hours during which all employees must be available.
example: Our core hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM for scheduling team meetings.

meaning: A system allowing employees to choose their start and finish work times.
example: Flex time allows him to drop his children at school before logging in.

meaning: The comfortable division of time and energy between work and personal life.
example: Working from home has greatly improved my work-life balance.

meaning: A culture where employees feel pressured to respond to messages at all hours.
example: To avoid always-on burnout, he turns off his work notifications at 6:00 PM.

meaning: Exhaustion resulting from excessive video conferencing or online meetings.
example: After six hours of calls today, I am suffering from serious Zoom fatigue.

meaning: An office setup where desks are not assigned and employees sit wherever is free.
example: Since we went hybrid, the company introduced hot-desking to save office space.

meaning: Doing only the minimum required tasks for your job without working extra hours.
example: Quiet quitting is often a sign that employees are feeling undervalued or burned out.

meaning: A virtual portal containing the digital tools employees need to do their jobs.
example: The HR department updated our digital workspace to include a new expense claim tool.

meaning: An agreement or trust established during a remote interaction or video call.
example: We closed the deal with a virtual handshake during our Zoom conference.

meaning: The amount of time spent looking at a digital screen like a computer or phone.
example: I try to reduce my screen time in the evening by reading physical books.

meaning: A person who grew up in the digital age and is very familiar with technology.
example: As a digital native, she easily learns how to use any new software app.

meaning: The process of welcoming and training a new employee in the company.
example: Our virtual onboarding includes video tutorials and chat introductions.

meaning: A virtual connection or session between a mentor and a less experienced worker.
example: They established a mentoring link through bi-weekly video calls.

meaning: A measure of how active and involved employees are in company chat channels.
example: The manager checks the engagement rate on team polls to see participation.

meaning: A central platform where documents, conversations, and tasks are integrated.
example: Microsoft Teams acts as the collaboration hub for our entire department.

meaning: Reserving a physical office desk in advance, usually via a mobile app.
example: Use the desk booking app to secure a workspace before coming into the office.

meaning: A scheduled day when remote employees are required to work in the office.
example: Wednesday is our team's anchor day, when we meet for planning.

meaning: The tendency of managers to favor employees who are physically present in the office.
example: To prevent proximity bias, our leaders evaluate everyone solely on output.

meaning: A meeting where group members suggest a large number of ideas to solve a problem.
example: We held a brainstorming session on Mural to collect design ideas.

meaning: Working together with others using digital platforms and online software.
example: Digital collaboration tools have allowed us to design products with global partners.

meaning: The practice of distributing tasks efficiently across team members.
example: The manager uses software for workload management to prevent employee burnout.
